paper_title: Characterization of marine diatom-infecting virus promoters in the model diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum

methods.description: This method determines whether the egfp gene with the tested promoters and the Sh ble gene with CffcpA pro. were introduced into colony-forming cells that showed resistance to Zeocin. Genomic PCR analyses were conducted using Tks Gflex DNA Polymerase, and the amplicons were analyzed using agarose gel electrophoresis.

steps.description: Regions containing the promoters and genes were amplified using the cells as a template.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify gene regions using cells as template

steps.description: The amplicons were analysed using agarose gel electrophoresis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze amplicons with agarose gel electrophoresis

methods.method_name: Effect of Culture Conditions on DIV Promoter Activity
methods.description: This method explores the effects of nutrient concentrations in media, growth phases of the tested strain, and light cycles on DIV promoter activity by culturing transformants in different conditions and analyzing mRNA levels.

steps.description: Two transformants with ClP1 were cultured in a low nutrient medium (f/10 medium) and standard nutrient medium (f/2 medium).
steps.searchable_summary: culture transformants in different nutrient media

steps.description: Transformants at the log and stationary growth phases were collected at approximately 13:30.
steps.searchable_summary: collect transformants at specific growth phases

steps.description: Total RNA samples from approximately 5 × 10^7 transformed cells were isolated using the above methods.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate total RNA from transformed cells

steps.description: Using qRT-PCR, we determined the abundance of egfp mRNA and that of rps mRNA.

methods.description: This method measures the eGFP fluorescence for the transformed cells using flow cytometry to evaluate cell sizes and eliminate debris from analysis.

steps.description: Approximately 1 × 10^4 cells were analysed using a BD LSRFortessa X-20 flow cytometer system equipped with BD FACS Diva software.
The cells were gated on endogenous chlorophyll fluorescence so that debris could be eliminated from the analysis conditions.

methods.description: Statistical analyses were performed on the activity levels of the promoters at the transcriptional and translational levels using Student's t-test.

steps.description: Statistical analyses were performed using Student's t-test for two group mean values.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Student's t-test on group mean values

methods.method_name: In Silico Analysis of Promoter Regions
methods.description: This method identifies putative cis-regulatory elements in potential DIV promoter regions and other extrinsic promoters using database searches and motif-finding algorithms.

steps.description: Putative cis-regulatory elements were identified by searching the PLACE and PlantCARE databases.
The promoterome analysis was performed following the method described by Russo et al.
A consensus sequence for the amplified regions of the tested promoters was analysed using the consensus motif-finding algorithms CONSENSUS from Melina II.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze consensus sequence using motif-finding algorithms
